data.title: Why Is Beyond Air Stock (XAIR) Up Today?
data.description:
Beyond Air (XAIR) stock rocketed higher on Tuesday after the company announced a deal between one of its subsidiaries and XTL Biopharmaceuticals (XLTB). Under the terms of this agreement, XTL Biopharmaceuticals has agreed to acquire 85% of NeuroNOS, a biotechnology company focused on the development of disease-modifying therapeutics targeting the core pathophysiology of Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) and neuro-oncology.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

XTL Biopharmaceuticals has taken an interest in NeuroNOS due to the growing number of children diagnosed with ASD. Current estimates are that roughly one in 31 children in the U.S. are affected by ASD, which is a major increase and has sparked government concern. Even so, there's no FDA-approved treatment for the underlying neurobiological mechanisms of ASD.

NeuroNOS' work to treat ASD has already resulted in two Orphan Drug Designations from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). These cover treatments for Phelan-McDermid Syndrome, a rare genetic disorder with strong autism correlation, and Glioblastoma, an incredibly aggressive and deadly form of brain cancer.

Beyond Air Stock Movement Today

Beyond Air stock was up 27.85% in pre-market trading on Tuesday, following a slight drop yesterday. The shares have rallied 20.66% year-to-date but have fallen 87.83% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of XAIR stock, as some 28 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of 6.04 million units.

Investors will also note that XTL Biopharmaceuticals stock surged 15.87% this morning alongside increased trading activity.
data.author: William White

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Trend Lower, All Eyes on Inflation Report, Bank Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures moved lower on Tuesday morning as traders awaited the key Consumer Price Index (CPI) reading for December 2025 and results from large U.S. banks for the fourth quarter of 2025. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.21%, 0.12%, and 0.21%, respectively, at 8:17 a.m. EST on January 13.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Monday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed in the green, with the S&P 500 rising 0.16%, the Dow gaining 0.17%, and the Nasdaq Composite advancing 0.26%.

Looking ahead, investors will closely watch today's CPI data to gain a greater understanding of inflationary trends and fill gaps left by last fall's extended U.S. government shutdown. Dow Jones consensus estimate indicates a 2.7% price rise over the twelve months through December. This figure aligns with November's lower-than-expected CPI data.

Turning to trending stocks, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) reported better-than-expected Q4 2025 earnings, driven by strength in the trading business. Shares of Intel (INTC) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) rose 3.4% and 2.7% in Tuesday's pre-market trading after KeyBanc upgraded both chipmakers to Buy, citing memory cycle and robust data center demand.

Meanwhile, Delta Air Lines (DAL) stock fell about 4% after reporting mixed Q4 2025 results. In contrast, shares of L3Harris Technologies (LHX) jumped more than 12% in pre-market trading after the defense contractor announced that it would pursue an initial public offering (IPO) of its missile solutions business in the second half of 2026 through a partnership with the U.S. Defense Department.

data.title: NVDA vs. AMD vs. AVGO: A Top Analyst Weighs the AI Chip Battle
data.description:
NVIDIA (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO) are all competing for position in the fast-growing AI chip market. Following updates from CES, top D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria reiterated a Buy rating on Nvidia and explained how its execution, costs, and overall platform strength compare with rivals like AMD and Broadcom.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Nvidia Outpaces AMD and Broadcom

At CES, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said the company's Vera Rubin platform is now in full production. Analyst Gil Luria noted that the system can cut AI token costs by up to ten times and train advanced models using far fewer GPUs. These gains make Nvidia's hardware more efficient and cheaper to operate, helping customers manage AI costs as workloads grow.

Beyond data centers, Nvidia is also pushing deeper into physical AI, including robotics and self-driving systems. As part of this effort, the company introduced the Alpamayo family of open-source models to speed up work in autonomous driving. Luria said this expansion gives Nvidia an added advantage, while AMD and Broadcom remain more focused on traditional data center and networking products.

While rivals continue to invest heavily in AI chips, Luria believes Nvidia's tight link between hardware, software, and developer tools keeps it ahead of the pack. As a result, he expects Nvidia to stay ahead of AMD and Broadcom for the "foreseeable future."

Which Chip Stock Is the Best Buy Right Now? 

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three stocks mentioned above, Nvidia offers the highest upside at about 43% and carries a Strong Buy rating from analysts. AMD follows with about 37% upside, and it also holds a Strong Buy rating.

Broadcom rounds out the group with a Strong Buy consensus as well, though its upside is lower at around 31% based on current analyst price targets.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: UK Lawsuit Filed Against Air India Adds New Legal Front for Tata Group after 2025 Plane Crash Tragedy
data.description:
The January 13, 2026, announcement of a High Court lawsuit in London has reignited investor concerns over the long-term financial liabilities facing Air India and its parent, the Tata Group. While the airline is privately held, the legal escalation is serving as a significant headwind for Tata Group's listed proxies, which are still recovering from the market volatility triggered by the June 2025 Ahmedabad crash.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Lawsuit Pressures Tata Group Market Cap

The filing of the lawsuit in the High Court on December 18 marks a transition from voluntary ex-gratia payments to litigated liability. Although Tata Sons has already pledged voluntary compensation of $120,000 (₹1 crore) per victim, the London court system allows for much higher damages based on loss of future earnings, which could dwarf the strict liability limits of the Montreal Convention.

Tata's Listed Proxies Are Under Scrutiny

Because Air India is not a listed entity, investors are tracking the Tata discount through its public subsidiaries. In the immediate aftermath of the crash and the subsequent legal filings, several group stocks have seen increased volatility:

  • Tata Technologies & Trent: These high-growth entities were among the biggest laggards, dropping over 2% as investors reassessed the group's private equity risk.
  • Indian Hotels Co. (IN:INDHOTEL): Shares dipped as analysts weighed whether the reputational blow to the Air India brand would spill over into the group's luxury hospitality segment.
  • Tata Motors (IN:TATAMOTORS): While structurally separate, the stock has become a sentiment bellwether for the Tata brand integrity in the face of safety investigations.

Tata Group Faces Insurance and Leasing Risks

Market analysts are closely watching the "hull and liability" premiums for Indian carriers. Following the 2025 disaster, insurance premiums are projected to rise by up to 100% in the next underwriting cycle.

For Air India, this could mean an annual cost jump from $28 million to over $50 million. Furthermore, the London lawsuit complicates the airline's credit profile, making future aircraft leasing and fleet expansion, critical to its turnaround plan, more expensive.

The Boeing-Honeywell Factor

A separate legal front in the United States could provide some financial relief for Air India if the blame shifts to manufacturing. Families are suing Boeing (BA) and Honeywell (HON) over allegedly faulty fuel switches that reportedly flipped to the cutoff position seconds after takeoff.

If the U.S. courts find Boeing liable, Air India could potentially subrogate its losses. However, if the London court finds Air India's pilot training or safety protocols were negligent, the airline could face uncapped damages under international law.

Investors watching the fallout can compare airline stocks on the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: L3Harris Stock (LHX) Thunders Higher on $1B Pentagon-Backed 'Arsenal of Freedom' IPO
data.description:
Shares in aerospace and defense technology company L3Harris Technologies (LHX) fired 13% higher in pre-market trading as it revealed plans for a Pentagon-backed IPO of its rocket motors business.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Unprecedented Speed and Scale

L3Harris hailed the "first-of-its-kind" proposed partnership with the Department of War to help ramp up the building of solid rocket motors to power vital U.S. and allied missiles.

Under the deal, the Department of War is investing in L3Harris' Missile Solutions business through a $1 billion convertible preferred security, which will automatically convert into equity in an IPO of the unit.

The listing of the Missile Solutions business, which includes programs such as Tomahawk and THAAD, is expected to take place in the second half of this year. This, L3Harris vows, will help create a public company "focused on delivering critical propulsion systems at unprecedented speed and scale."

"We're taking action to build today's 'Arsenal of Freedom' by launching a pure-play missile solutions provider," said Christopher Kubasik, Chairman and CEO of L3Harris. "Recent Trump Administration actions have placed renewed emphasis on strengthening the defense industrial base and reinvigorating competition following a 30-year wave of consolidation. This new company will serve as a key partner to the DoW in supporting efforts to deter and defeat America's adversaries."

Potential Conflicts Loom

Pentagon officials have reportedly spent most of the past year urging weapons makers to dramatically increase missile production rates to better prepare for a potential future conflict with China. This mood has helped the L3Harris share price perform strongly over the last 12 months – see below:
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/13/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Slip Ahead of CPI Data, Big Bank Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down early Tuesday as traders anticipated key Consumer Price Index (CPI) data and big bank results for Q4. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.23%, 0.14%, and 0.12%, respectively, at 3:45 a.m. EST on January 13.

During Monday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed in the green, with the S&P 500 rising 0.16%, the Dow gaining 0.17%, and the Nasdaq Composite advancing 0.26%.

Looking ahead, investors will closely watch today's CPI data to gain greater understanding of inflationary trends and fill gaps from last fall's extended U.S. government shutdown. Dow Jones consensus estimates anticipate a 2.7% price rise over the twelve months through December. This figure aligns with November's lower-than-expected CPI results.

Moreover, banking giant JPMorgan (JPM) will kick off the Q4 earnings season today, joined by airline leader Delta Air Lines (DAL).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.19%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $59.82 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,589 per ounce on Tuesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed amid persistent geopolitical tensions involving Iran and Greenland. U.S. President Donald Trump stated last night that any country doing business with Iran will face a 25% tariff on all their business with the U.S.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on January 13 amid Iran's ongoing geopolitical unrest.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 0.90%. In China, the Shanghai Composite fell 0.64%, while the Shenzhen Component added 1.44%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei surged 3.10%, and the Topix added 2.41%.
